adams_williamson_equation.name: Adams-Williamson Equation
adams_williamson_equation.caption:
Which equation reveals Earth's interior structure — how pressure and density change with depth — without ever digging? 🌍
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A cornerstone of geophysics and seismology, it connects how seismic wave speeds change with depth to the density profile of our planet — letting scientists map the crust, mantle and core, and probe the pressure deep inside a self-gravitating world without drilling a single hole.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

advection_equation.name: Advection Equation
advection_equation.caption:
Which equation tracks how a quantity is transported through space — pollution drifting on the wind, salt riding ocean currents? 🌊
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A foundation of fluid dynamics and transport theory, it models how a scalar field is carried by a flow field, underpinning weather prediction, ocean modelling, atmospheric dispersion and computational fluid dynamics.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

alfven_velocity.name: Alfvén Velocity
alfven_velocity.caption:
Which equation sets the speed of magnetic waves rippling through a plasma? ⚡
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

A key result in plasma physics and magnetohydrodynamics, it links magnetic field strength and plasma density to wave speed — essential for understanding solar flares, the solar wind, auroras and magnetic confinement in fusion reactors.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

ampere_law_v1.name: Ampère's Law (Version 1)
ampere_law_v1.caption:
Which equation says electric currents create the magnetic fields inside every motor and generator? 🧲
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

One of the four pillars of classical electromagnetism, this differential form relates the curl of the magnetic field to current density, underpinning electric motors, transformers, inductors and the whole theory of electromagnetic fields.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

ampere_law_v2.name: Ampère's Law (Version 2)
ampere_law_v2.caption:
Which equation links the magnetic field looping around a wire to the current threading through it? 🧲
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

In this integral form of classical electromagnetism, the line integral of the magnetic field around a closed loop equals the enclosed current — the workhorse for calculating fields in solenoids, coaxial cables and current-carrying conductors.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

angular_frequency.name: Angular Frequency
angular_frequency.caption:
Which equation converts how many cycles per second into radians per second for anything that oscillates? 🔄
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

A staple of oscillations and wave physics, it converts an oscillator’s cycle rate into radians swept per second, describing pendulums, AC circuits, springs and every kind of periodic and rotational motion.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

angular_impulse.name: Angular Impulse
angular_impulse.caption:
Which equation says a twist applied over time changes how much an object spins? 🌀
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A core result in rotational dynamics, it links the torque applied over a time interval to the change in spin, the rotational cousin of linear impulse used across mechanics and engineering.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

angular_jerk.name: Angular Jerk
angular_jerk.caption:
Which equation measures how quickly angular acceleration itself is changing during a spin? 🌀
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A higher-order quantity in rotational kinematics, it is the rate of change of angular acceleration — relevant to machinery, robotics and the smoothness of any accelerating rotation.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

angular_momentum_cross_product.name: Angular Momentum (Cross Product Form)
angular_momentum_cross_product.caption:
Which equation captures an object's rotational motion as the cross product of position and momentum? 🌀
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

A fundamental conserved quantity in classical and quantum mechanics, defined as the vector product of position and linear momentum — governing orbits, spinning tops, planetary motion and the stability of everything that rotates.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

angular_momentum_rotational.name: Angular Momentum (Rotational Form)
angular_momentum_rotational.caption:
Which equation gives a rigid body's spin as its resistance-to-rotation times how fast it turns? 🌀
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A central result in rotational dynamics, expressing spin as the product of the moment of inertia and angular velocity — the reason a figure skater speeds up when pulling their arms in, and a conserved quantity throughout mechanics.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

angular_velocity.name: Angular Velocity
angular_velocity.caption:
Which equation describes how fast an object rotates, measured in radians swept per second? 🔄
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

The rotational counterpart of linear speed, it quantifies the rate of change of angular position — foundational to rotational dynamics, orbital mechanics and the physics of gears, turbines and spinning bodies.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

archimedes_principle.name: Archimedes' Principle
archimedes_principle.caption:
Which equation explains why ships float — the upward push equals the weight of fluid pushed aside? 🚢
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

A foundational result in fluid statics, the buoyant force on a submerged body equals the weight of the displaced fluid — explaining flotation, hot-air balloons, submarines and hydrometers.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

arrhenius_equation.name: Arrhenius Equation
arrhenius_equation.caption:
Which equation explains why chemical reactions speed up dramatically as you heat things up? 🔥
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A cornerstone of chemical kinetics, it links reaction rate to temperature and activation energy through an exponential factor — describing everything from cooking and corrosion to enzyme activity and battery degradation.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

barometric_formula.name: Barometric Formula
barometric_formula.caption:
Which equation predicts how air pressure falls exponentially as you climb higher into the atmosphere? ⛰️
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A key result in atmospheric physics, it describes how pressure decreases with altitude in a gravity field, underpinning altimeters, weather balloons, aviation and models of planetary atmospheres.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

bekenstein_hawking_entropy.name: Bekenstein-Hawking Entropy
bekenstein_hawking_entropy.caption:
Which equation says a black hole's entropy is set by the area of its event horizon, not its volume? 🕳️
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

A landmark of black hole thermodynamics, it ties entropy to horizon area and hints at a deep link between gravity, quantum theory and information — a cornerstone of the holographic principle and quantum gravity research.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

bernoulli_equation.name: Bernoulli's Equation
bernoulli_equation.caption:
Which equation says faster-moving fluid has lower pressure — the idea behind how wings generate lift? ✈️
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A classic of fluid dynamics, it relates a fluid's pressure, speed and height along a streamline via energy conservation — explaining aerofoil lift, venturi meters, carburettors and flow through pipes.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

bohr_model.name: Bohr Model Equation
bohr_model.caption:
Which equation gives the quantised energy levels of hydrogen, explaining why atoms emit sharp spectral lines? ⚛️
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

An early triumph of quantum theory, it predicts the discrete energy levels of the hydrogen atom and the wavelengths of its spectral lines, bridging classical orbits and modern atomic physics.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

boltzmann_distribution.name: Boltzmann Distribution
boltzmann_distribution.caption:
Which equation tells you how likely a system is to occupy a state of given energy at a given temperature? 🌡️
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

A foundation of statistical mechanics, it gives the probability of a state as an exponential in energy over temperature — underpinning gas behaviour, reaction rates, semiconductors and the meaning of temperature itself.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

boltzmann_entropy.name: Boltzmann Entropy
boltzmann_entropy.caption:
Which equation connects entropy to the number of microscopic arrangements a system can have? 🎲
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A profound bridge between the microscopic and macroscopic, it defines entropy in terms of the count of accessible microstates — the statistical heart of the second law of thermodynamics, famously carved on its discoverer's gravestone.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

borda_carnot_equation.name: Borda-Carnot Equation
borda_carnot_equation.caption:
Which equation estimates the energy lost when a fluid flow suddenly expands or hits an obstacle? 🌊
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A practical result in fluid mechanics, it quantifies head loss due to abrupt changes in flow, used in pipe network design, hydraulics and analysing turbulence-driven energy dissipation.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

born_rule.name: Born Rule
born_rule.caption:
Which equation turns a quantum wavefunction into the probability of actually measuring a result? 🎲
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

A postulate at the core of quantum mechanics, it states that the probability of an outcome is the squared magnitude of the wavefunction amplitude — the link between quantum formalism and what detectors actually record.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

capacitance.name: Capacitance
capacitance.caption:
Which equation measures how much electric charge a component can store per volt applied? 🔋
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A basic definition in electronics and electromagnetism, relating stored charge to voltage — fundamental to capacitors, filters, timing circuits, touchscreens and energy storage.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

capacitive_reactance.name: Capacitive Reactance
capacitive_reactance.caption:
Which equation describes how a capacitor resists alternating current more strongly at low frequencies? 🔌
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A key relation in AC circuit theory, it gives the frequency-dependent opposition a capacitor presents to alternating current — central to filters, audio crossovers, power electronics and signal processing.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

cauchy_momentum_equation_v1.name: Cauchy Momentum Equation (Version 1)
cauchy_momentum_equation_v1.caption:
Which equation expresses Newton's second law for a continuous deforming material, in convective form? 🌊
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

A general statement of momentum conservation in continuum mechanics, relating acceleration of a material element to internal stresses and body forces — the parent of the fluid-flow equations used across engineering and geophysics.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

cauchy_momentum_equation_v2.name: Cauchy Momentum Equation (Version 2)
cauchy_momentum_equation_v2.caption:
Which equation states momentum balance for a deforming continuum using the stress tensor divergence? 🌊
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

In this form, continuum mechanics expresses how the divergence of the stress tensor and body forces drive the motion of a material — the foundation from which fluid and solid dynamics equations are derived.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

centrifugal_force.name: Centrifugal Force
centrifugal_force.caption:
Which equation gives the outward-seeming push you feel when a car whips around a tight bend? 🌀
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

An inertial effect in rotating reference frames, it depends on mass, rotation rate and radius — explaining spin dryers, centrifuges, banked roads and the apparent forces in non-inertial frames.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

centripetal_acceleration.name: Centripetal Acceleration
centripetal_acceleration.caption:
Which equation gives the inward acceleration that keeps anything moving in a circle? 🔄
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

A fundamental result in circular motion, it depends on speed and radius and points toward the centre — governing orbits, banked curves, spinning machinery and satellites held in their paths.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

charles_law.name: Charles' Law
charles_law.caption:
Which gas equation says heat a trapped gas at fixed pressure and its volume grows in step with temperature? 🎈
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A classic gas relation in thermodynamics, it states that volume is proportional to absolute temperature at constant pressure — explaining hot-air balloons, thermometers and the behaviour of gases when heated.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

continuity_equation.name: Continuity Equation
continuity_equation.caption:
Which equation says whatever flows in must flow out — mass is conserved as a fluid moves? 🌊
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A statement of local conservation in fluid dynamics and electromagnetism, linking the change of density to the divergence of flux — the reason a narrowing pipe speeds up flow, and a backbone of transport theory.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

coulombs_law.name: Coulomb's Law
coulombs_law.caption:
Which equation gives the force between two electric charges, falling off with the square of their separation? ⚡
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

A foundation of electrostatics, it says the force between charges scales with their product and inversely with distance squared — governing everything from static cling to the structure of atoms and molecules.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

crackle_fifth_derivative.name: Crackle (Fifth Derivative)
crackle_fifth_derivative.caption:
Which quantity is the fifth time-derivative of position — beyond velocity, acceleration, jerk and snap? 🎢
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A higher-order kinematic quantity, the fifth derivative of position with respect to time, relevant to motion-control, cam design and analysing extremely smooth or jerk-sensitive systems.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

dalembert_operator.name: d'Alembert Operator
dalembert_operator.caption:
Which operator is the spacetime version of the Laplacian, driving every wave in relativistic physics? 🌐
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A central object in field theory and special relativity, it combines space and time derivatives to describe wave propagation in a Lorentz-invariant way — appearing in electromagnetism, quantum field theory and gravitational waves.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

daltons_law.name: Dalton's Law
daltons_law.caption:
Which equation says the total pressure of a gas mixture is just the sum of each gas's own pressure? 🫧
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

A key relation in the physics of gas mixtures, stating that total pressure equals the sum of partial pressures — essential to respiration, scuba diving, atmospheric chemistry and gas-phase reactions.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

darcy_law.name: Darcy's Law
darcy_law.caption:
Which equation predicts how fast water seeps through soil, sand or porous rock? 💧
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

The foundation of hydrogeology and porous-media flow, relating flow rate to permeability and pressure gradient — governing groundwater movement, oil reservoirs, filtration and aquifer management.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

darcy_weisbach_equation.name: Darcy-Weisbach Equation
darcy_weisbach_equation.caption:
Which equation tells engineers how much pressure is lost to friction as fluid flows down a pipe? 🚰
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A workhorse of hydraulics, it relates head loss to pipe length, diameter, flow speed and a friction factor — central to designing water networks, pipelines and HVAC systems.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

de_broglie_relation.name: de Broglie Relation
de_broglie_relation.caption:
Which equation says every particle also behaves as a wave, with a wavelength set by its momentum? 🌊
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

A revolutionary idea in quantum mechanics, linking a particle's momentum to a wavelength — the basis of electron microscopy, matter-wave interference and the wave-particle duality at the heart of the quantum world.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

digamma_function.name: Digamma Function
digamma_function.caption:
Which special function is the logarithmic derivative of the factorial-generalising gamma function? ➗
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

An important special function in analysis and number theory, defined as the derivative of the log of the gamma function — appearing in series summation, statistical distributions and evaluating tricky integrals.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

distance_modulus.name: Distance Modulus
distance_modulus.caption:
Which astronomy equation turns how bright a star looks versus how bright it truly is into its distance? 🔭
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A fundamental tool in observational astronomy, relating apparent and absolute magnitude to distance — the basis of the cosmic distance ladder used to measure how far away stars and galaxies lie.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

doppler_effect.name: Doppler Effect
doppler_effect.caption:
Which equation explains why a siren's pitch drops as it speeds past you? 🚑
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

A wave phenomenon central to physics and astronomy, describing how observed frequency shifts with relative motion — behind radar guns, medical ultrasound, and measuring the motion of stars and galaxies.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

drag_equation.name: Drag Equation
drag_equation.caption:
Which equation gives the resistive force a moving object feels as it pushes through air or water? 🪂
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A key result in fluid dynamics, relating this resistive force to fluid density, speed squared, area and a shape-dependent coefficient — governing skydivers, car aerodynamics, aircraft design and terminal velocity.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

drift_velocity.name: Drift Velocity
drift_velocity.caption:
Which equation gives the surprisingly slow average speed of electrons carrying a current down a wire? 🔌
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A concept in solid-state and electrical physics, giving the net speed of charge carriers under an applied field — explaining conduction, current density and how metals and semiconductors carry electricity.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

einstein_field_equations_no_lambda_v1.name: Einstein Field Equations (No Lambda, Version 1)
einstein_field_equations_no_lambda_v1.caption:
Which equation says matter and energy tell spacetime how to curve, and curvature tells matter how to move? 🌌
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

The heart of general relativity, relating spacetime curvature to the energy and momentum it contains — predicting black holes, gravitational lensing, gravitational waves and the expansion of the universe.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

einstein_field_equations_no_lambda_v2.name: Einstein Field Equations (No Lambda, Version 2)
einstein_field_equations_no_lambda_v2.caption:
Which equation ties the geometry of spacetime directly to its energy-momentum content, in trace-reversed form? 🌌
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A compact form of general relativity's gravitational equations, expressing how energy and momentum source spacetime curvature — the mathematical engine behind modern cosmology and gravitational physics.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

einstein_field_equations_with_lambda_v1.name: Einstein Field Equations (With Lambda, Version 1)
einstein_field_equations_with_lambda_v1.caption:
Which equation adds a cosmological constant to gravity, encoding the dark energy accelerating the universe? 🌌
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

The equations of general relativity extended with a cosmological constant, linking spacetime curvature to energy content plus a vacuum term — the framework behind the accelerating expansion and modern cosmology.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

einstein_field_equations_with_lambda_v2.name: Einstein Field Equations (With Lambda, Version 2)
einstein_field_equations_with_lambda_v2.caption:
Which equation of gravity carries the vacuum-energy term thought to drive cosmic acceleration? 🌌
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

A form of general relativity's gravitational equations retaining the cosmological constant, connecting geometry to energy-momentum and dark energy — central to the standard model of cosmology.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

einstein_relation_diffusion.name: Einstein Relation (Diffusion)
einstein_relation_diffusion.caption:
Which equation links how easily particles drift to how vigorously they randomly diffuse? 🌡️
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A cornerstone of statistical physics, connecting mobility and diffusion through temperature — a fluctuation-dissipation result underpinning Brownian motion, colloids, semiconductors and electrochemistry.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

electrical_power.name: Electrical Power
electrical_power.caption:
Which equation tells you the rate at which a device converts electrical energy — volts times amps? 💡
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A fundamental relation in electrical engineering, giving this rate as the product of voltage and current — the basis of energy bills, appliance ratings, circuit design and electricity distribution.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

energy_momentum_relation.name: Energy-Momentum Relation
energy_momentum_relation.caption:
Which relativistic equation ties a particle's total energy to its momentum and rest mass? ⚡
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

A pillar of special relativity, generalising mass-energy equivalence to moving particles — essential in particle physics, describing photons, relativistic collisions and the kinematics at accelerators.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

enthalpy.name: Enthalpy
enthalpy.caption:
Which thermodynamic quantity bundles a system's internal energy with its pressure-volume work? 🔥
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A central concept in thermodynamics and chemistry, tracking heat exchanged at constant pressure — the basis of reaction heats, calorimetry, phase changes and chemical engineering energy balances.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

escape_velocity.name: Escape Velocity
escape_velocity.caption:
Which equation gives the minimum speed needed to break free of a planet's gravity forever? 🚀
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A classic result in gravitation and orbital mechanics, depending on a body's mass and radius — governing rocket launches, planetary atmospheres retaining gases, and the threshold for leaving a gravity well.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

eulers_formula.name: Euler's Formula
eulers_formula.caption:
Which equation links the exponential of an imaginary number to sine and cosine — the bridge to complex analysis? 🌀
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

A profound identity connecting exponential growth to circular motion, unifying complex exponentials with trigonometry — indispensable in signal processing, Fourier analysis, quantum mechanics and electrical engineering.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

eulers_identity.name: Euler's Identity
eulers_identity.caption:
Which single line unites five fundamental constants and is often voted mathematics' most beautiful equation? ✨
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A celebrated special case linking the exponential, imaginary unit, pi, one and zero in one elegant relation — a striking demonstration of the deep unity between analysis, geometry and algebra.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

eyring_equation.name: Eyring Equation
eyring_equation.caption:
Which equation predicts reaction rates from transition-state theory — the quantum upgrade to older rate laws? ⚗️
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A foundation of chemical kinetics, deriving reaction rate constants from the thermodynamics of the transition state — used across physical chemistry, enzymology and materials science to model how fast reactions proceed.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

faradays_law_v1.name: Faraday's Law (Version 1)
faradays_law_v1.caption:
Which equation says a changing magnetic field creates a voltage — the principle behind every generator? ⚡
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

A pillar of electromagnetism, stating that a changing magnetic flux induces an electromotive force — the basis of electric generators, transformers, induction cooktops and wireless charging.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

faradays_law_v2.name: Faraday's Law (Version 2)
faradays_law_v2.caption:
Which equation relates the curl of the electric field to a changing magnetic field over time? ⚡
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

In differential form, this law of electromagnetism ties the swirl of the electric field to the rate of change of the magnetic field — one of the four equations unifying electricity, magnetism and light.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

fick_first_law.name: Fick's First Law
fick_first_law.caption:
Which equation says particles flow from crowded regions to empty ones, driven by concentration differences? 💨
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

The foundation of diffusion theory, relating particle flux to the concentration gradient — governing gas exchange in lungs, drug delivery, semiconductor doping and mixing in fluids.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

fick_second_law.name: Fick's Second Law
fick_second_law.caption:
Which equation predicts how a concentration profile spreads and evolves over time as things diffuse? 💨
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

A partial differential equation at the core of diffusion and transport theory, describing how concentration changes in time and space — key to heat treatment of metals, battery design and biological transport.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

first_law_thermodynamics_v1.name: First Law of Thermodynamics (Version 1)
first_law_thermodynamics_v1.caption:
Which equation is energy conservation for heat engines — internal energy changes by heat added minus work done? 🔥
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A statement of energy conservation for heat and work, requiring that a system's internal energy changes only through heat exchange and work done — governing engines, refrigerators, chemical reactions and the impossibility of perpetual motion.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

first_law_thermodynamics_v2.name: First Law of Thermodynamics (Version 2)
first_law_thermodynamics_v2.caption:
Which equation, in differential form, balances tiny changes in heat, work and internal energy? 🔥
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A differential statement of energy conservation in thermodynamics, connecting infinitesimal heat and work to changes in internal energy — the starting point for deriving thermodynamic relations and cycles.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

fouriers_law.name: Fourier's Law
fouriers_law.caption:
Which equation says heat flows from hot to cold at a rate set by the temperature gradient? 🌡️
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

The foundation of heat conduction, relating heat flux to the temperature gradient and thermal conductivity — essential to insulation, electronics cooling, building design and thermal engineering.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

free_fall_equation.name: Free Fall Equation
free_fall_equation.caption:
Which equation predicts how far an object drops under gravity as time ticks on? 🍎
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A classic of kinematics, describing motion under constant gravitational acceleration — the basis of projectile motion, falling-body experiments and introductory mechanics.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

gamma_function.name: Gamma Function
gamma_function.caption:
Which function extends the factorial to fractional and complex numbers — what is 'half factorial'? ➗
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A cornerstone special function in analysis, generalising the factorial beyond whole numbers — appearing throughout probability, complex analysis, number theory and the solutions of differential equations.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

gauss_law_electricity_integral.name: Gauss' Law - Electricity (Integral Form)
gauss_law_electricity_integral.caption:
Which equation says the electric flux through a closed surface reveals the charge trapped inside? ⚡
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

In integral form, this law of electromagnetism links the total electric flux through a closed surface to the enclosed charge — a powerful shortcut for fields of symmetric charge distributions.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

gauss_law_electricity_v1.name: Gauss' Law - Electricity (Version 1)
gauss_law_electricity_v1.caption:
Which equation ties the divergence of the electric field directly to local charge density? ⚡
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

In differential form, one of the four laws of electromagnetism, stating that electric charge is the source of diverging electric field lines — foundational to electrostatics and field theory.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

gauss_law_electricity_v2.name: Gauss' Law - Electricity (Version 2)
gauss_law_electricity_v2.caption:
Which fundamental law states electric field lines spring from charge, written via field divergence? ⚡
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A member of the four equations of classical electromagnetism, relating the divergence of the electric field to charge density — the basis for understanding capacitors, conductors and electric fields in matter.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

gauss_law_electricity_v3.name: Gauss' Law - Electricity (Version 3)
gauss_law_electricity_v3.caption:
Which of the four great laws of electromagnetism connects electric flux to enclosed charge? ⚡
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

A statement that electric charge sources the electric field, expressed through flux and the electric displacement field — central to electromagnetism in dielectrics and materials.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

gauss_law_gravity_v1.name: Gauss' Law - Gravity (Version 1)
gauss_law_gravity_v1.caption:
Which equation is the gravitational twin of the electric flux law, tying field to enclosed mass? 🌍
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A reformulation of gravitation paralleling electromagnetism, relating the flux of the gravitational field to the mass it encloses — a neat tool for computing fields of spheres, shells and planets.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

gauss_law_gravity_v2.name: Gauss' Law - Gravity (Version 2)
gauss_law_gravity_v2.caption:
Which equation states that mass density is the source of a diverging gravitational field? 🌍
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

In differential form, this law of gravitation ties the divergence of the gravitational field to mass density — a compact way to express Newtonian gravity used in geophysics and astrophysics.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

gauss_law_magnetism_differential_v1.name: Gauss' Law - Magnetism (Differential, Version 1)
gauss_law_magnetism_differential_v1.caption:
Which equation encodes the fact that isolated magnetic north or south poles have never been found? 🧲
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

One of the four laws of electromagnetism, stating the magnetic field has zero divergence — the mathematical expression that magnetic monopoles do not appear, so field lines always close on themselves.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

gauss_law_magnetism_differential_v2.name: Gauss' Law - Magnetism (Differential, Version 2)
gauss_law_magnetism_differential_v2.caption:
Which law says magnetic field lines never begin or end — there are no magnetic monopoles? 🧲
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A pillar of classical electromagnetism, requiring the magnetic field to have no sources or sinks — the reason every magnet has paired poles and field lines form closed loops.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

gauss_law_magnetism_integral.name: Gauss' Law - Magnetism (Integral Form)
gauss_law_magnetism_integral.caption:
Which equation says the net magnetic flux through any closed surface is always exactly zero? 🧲
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

In integral form, this electromagnetism law states that magnetic flux entering a closed surface equals the flux leaving — the signature of a field with no monopole sources.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

gravitational_force_v1.name: Gravitational Force (Version 1)
gravitational_force_v1.caption:
Which equation says every mass attracts every other, weakening with the square of the distance? 🌍
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

The universal law of gravitation, giving the attractive force between two masses in terms of their product and separation squared — governing falling apples, planetary orbits, tides and the motion of galaxies.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

gravitational_force_v2.name: Gravitational Force (Version 2)
gravitational_force_v2.caption:
Which equation, in vector form, gives the attractive pull between two masses along the line joining them? 🌍
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A vector statement of universal gravitation, capturing both the strength and direction of attraction between masses — the foundation of celestial mechanics, satellite motion and orbital dynamics.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

gyroscopic_precession.name: Gyroscopic Precession
gyroscopic_precession.caption:
Which equation explains why a spinning top slowly circles instead of simply toppling over? 🌀
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A result in rotational dynamics, relating the slow circling of a spin axis to torque and angular momentum — behind gyroscopes, spinning tops, bicycle stability and the slow wobble of the spinning Earth.

hagen_poiseuille_equation.name: Hagen-Poiseuille Equation
hagen_poiseuille_equation.caption:
Which equation shows that halving a pipe's radius cuts flow to a sixteenth — why narrow tubes throttle flow? 🩸
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

A key result in fluid dynamics, relating flow rate through a tube to pressure, viscosity and the fourth power of radius — governing blood flow, microfluidics and viscous flow in narrow channels.

half_life.name: Half-Life
half_life.caption:
Which equation describes exponential decay — the time for half of a radioactive sample to disappear? ☢️
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A fundamental relation in nuclear physics, describing how unstable nuclei decay exponentially over time — the basis of radiometric dating, medical imaging, nuclear power and pharmacokinetics.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

half_life_copy.name: Half-Life (Second Form)
half_life_copy.caption:
Which equation captures exponential decay — the time for half of an unstable sample to break down? ☢️
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A core relation in nuclear and physical science, describing exponential decay of unstable populations over time — underpinning carbon dating, radiotracers, drug clearance and nuclear physics.

harish_chandra_formula.name: Harish-Chandra Formula
harish_chandra_formula.caption:
Which deep formula from representation theory computes characters on semisimple Lie groups? ➗
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

An advanced result in pure mathematics and representation theory, giving explicit character formulas for Lie groups — foundational to harmonic analysis, mathematical physics and the study of symmetry.

hawking_temperature.name: Hawking Temperature
hawking_temperature.caption:
Which equation says black holes aren't truly black — they glow with a faint thermal radiation? 🕳️

A landmark of black hole thermodynamics, giving the temperature of radiation emitted from an event horizon via quantum effects — a rare bridge between gravity, quantum theory and thermodynamics.

heisenberg_uncertainty_energy_time.name: Heisenberg Uncertainty - Energy/Time
heisenberg_uncertainty_energy_time.caption:
Which principle limits how precisely energy and time can be known together, allowing fleeting virtual particles? ⚛️
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A fundamental limit in quantum mechanics tying the spread in energy to that in time — explaining natural linewidths, virtual particles and the fuzzy energy of short-lived quantum states.

heisenberg_uncertainty_position_momentum.name: Heisenberg Uncertainty - Position/Momentum

Which principle says the more precisely you pin a particle's position, the fuzzier its momentum becomes? ⚛️

A cornerstone of quantum mechanics setting a hard limit on simultaneously knowing position and momentum — a consequence of wave-particle duality that shapes atomic structure and quantum technology.

helmholtz_free_energy_v1.name: Helmholtz Free Energy (Version 1)
helmholtz_free_energy_v1.caption:
Which thermodynamic potential tells you the useful work available from a system at constant temperature? 🔥
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A key potential in thermodynamics and statistical mechanics, combining internal energy and entropy at fixed temperature — governing spontaneity, phase transitions and equilibrium in physical chemistry.

helmholtz_free_energy_v2.name: Helmholtz Free Energy (Version 2)
helmholtz_free_energy_v2.caption:
Which quantity balances internal energy against temperature times entropy to predict equilibrium? 🔥
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A thermodynamic potential minimised at equilibrium for systems held at constant temperature and volume — central to statistical mechanics, materials science and the study of phase behaviour.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

hookes_law.name: Hooke's Law
hookes_law.caption:
Which equation says a spring's restoring force grows in proportion to how far you stretch it? 🌀
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

A foundation of elasticity and mechanics, stating that force is proportional to displacement for small deformations — behind springs, elastic materials, vibrations and structural engineering.

ideal_gas_law.name: Ideal Gas Law
ideal_gas_law.caption:
Which equation links pressure, volume, temperature and amount for a simple gas in one tidy relation? 🎈

A central result in thermodynamics, tying together pressure, volume, temperature and number of moles for a dilute gas — the workhorse for chemistry, engines, weather and countless engineering calculations.

impulse_v1.name: Impulse (Version 1)
impulse_v1.caption:
Which equation says a force applied over time changes an object's momentum — why airbags save lives? 🚗
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A core result in mechanics, equating the force-time product to the change in momentum — explaining collisions, crumple zones, cushioning and the physics of catching a fast ball.

impulse_v2.name: Impulse (Version 2)
impulse_v2.caption:
Which equation integrates force over a time interval to give the total change in momentum? 🚗
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

In integral form, this mechanics relation sums a varying force over time to yield the momentum change — essential to impact analysis, rocketry and sports biomechanics.

jurin_law_capillary_rise.name: Jurin's Law - Capillary Rise

Which equation predicts how high water climbs up a thin tube against gravity, all by itself? 💧
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A result in fluid statics and surface science, relating how high a liquid climbs to surface tension, tube radius and contact angle — explaining how plants draw water up and how liquids wick through porous materials.

kepler_third_law.name: Kepler's Third Law

Which equation ties a planet's orbital period to the size of its orbit — the rhythm of the solar system? 🪐
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A pillar of orbital mechanics, relating the square of a planet's period to the cube of its orbital radius — the basis for weighing stars, discovering exoplanets and planning spacecraft trajectories.

kinetic_energy.name: Kinetic Energy

Which equation gives the energy an object carries purely because it's moving — scaling with speed squared? 🏃

A fundamental quantity in mechanics, depending on mass and the square of speed — central to collisions, work-energy analysis, vehicle safety and energy in every moving system.

kronecker_delta.name: Kronecker Delta

Which mathematical symbol equals one when two indices match and zero otherwise — the identity in disguise? ➗

A compact tool across linear algebra, tensor calculus and physics, encoding the identity matrix and orthogonality — indispensable for index notation, quantum mechanics and continuum mechanics.

lane_emden_equation.name: Lane-Emden Equation

Which equation models the internal structure of a self-gravitating star as a balance of gravity and pressure? ⭐

A key result in stellar astrophysics, describing how density and pressure vary inside a self-gravitating gas sphere — the basis of polytropic stellar models and understanding stellar interiors.

langevin_equation.name: Langevin Equation

Which equation captures a particle jostled randomly by countless molecular kicks, plus friction? 🎲

A foundational stochastic differential equation in statistical physics, combining a systematic drag force with random noise — modelling Brownian motion, colloids, molecular dynamics and thermal fluctuations.

laplace_equation_v1.name: Laplace's Equation (Version 1)
laplace_equation_v1.caption:
Which equation describes steady states with no sources — where a potential smoothly averages its surroundings? 🌐

A central partial differential equation in physics and engineering, whose solutions are the harmonic functions describing electrostatic potentials, steady heat, fluid flow and gravitational fields.

laplace_equation_v2.name: Laplace's Equation (Version 2)
laplace_equation_v2.caption:
Which equation sets the divergence of a gradient to zero, governing equilibrium potentials everywhere? 🌐
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A workhorse partial differential equation whose smooth, source-free solutions appear in electrostatics, incompressible flow, steady heat conduction and potential theory across physics.

laplace_transform.name: Laplace Transform
laplace_transform.caption:
Which integral transform turns hard differential equations into simple algebra in a new variable? ➗

A powerful tool in applied mathematics and engineering, converting differential equations into algebraic ones — indispensable for control systems, circuit analysis and solving initial-value problems.

lorentz_force_v1.name: Lorentz Force (Version 1)

Which equation gives the combined push on a charged particle from electric and magnetic fields? ⚡
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A cornerstone of electromagnetism, giving the force on a moving charge from electric and magnetic fields — behind particle accelerators, mass spectrometers, electric motors and the aurora.

lorentz_force_v2.name: Lorentz Force (Version 2)

Which equation, in field form, gives the force density on charges and currents in a field? ⚡
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A statement of the force exerted by electromagnetic fields on charge and current distributions — foundational to plasma physics, magnetohydrodynamics and the design of electromagnetic devices.

luminosity_v1.name: Luminosity (Version 1)

Which equation gives the total power a star radiates from its temperature and size? ⭐

A key relation in astrophysics, linking a star's total radiated power to its radius and surface temperature — central to the Hertzsprung-Russell diagram, stellar classification and measuring cosmic distances.

luminosity_v2.name: Luminosity (Version 2)
luminosity_v2.caption:
Which equation connects the brightness we receive from a star to its true power and its distance? ⭐
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A fundamental astronomy relation tying observed flux to a star’s intrinsic radiated power and distance via the inverse-square law — the basis for the cosmic distance ladder and standard candles.

majorana_equation.name: Majorana Equation

Which relativistic equation describes a fermion that is its very own antiparticle? ⚛️
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A relativistic wave equation in particle physics for particles identical to their antiparticles — central to neutrino physics, searches for neutrinoless double beta decay and beyond-standard-model theory.

malus_law.name: Malus' Law
malus_law.caption:
Which equation predicts how much light gets through a polariser as you rotate it? 🕶️

A foundational result in optics, giving transmitted intensity through a polariser as the cosine squared of the angle — behind polarised sunglasses, LCD screens and photoelasticity.

mass_energy_equivalence.name: Mass-Energy Equivalence
mass_energy_equivalence.caption:
Which equation reveals that mass and energy are two faces of the same thing — powering stars and reactors? 💥
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

Perhaps the most famous relation in physics, stating that mass and energy are interconvertible — underlying nuclear power, stellar fusion, particle-antiparticle creation and the energy budget of the cosmos.

Which quantity measures how hard it is to change an object's spin, depending on how its mass is spread out? 🌀

A key concept in rotational dynamics, quantifying resistance to angular acceleration based on mass distribution — the rotational analogue of mass, essential to flywheels, turbines and spinning bodies.

newton_law_viscosity.caption:
Which equation relates the drag between fluid layers to how fast the flow changes across them? 🌊
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

The defining relation for a thick, syrupy fluid, linking shear stress to the velocity gradient through the fluid’s internal friction — foundational to lubrication, blood flow, polymer processing and fluid mechanics.

partition_function.name: Partition Function
partition_function.caption:
Which quantity sums over all a system's states and unlocks every thermodynamic property from statistics? 🎲

The central object of statistical mechanics, encoding how energy is distributed across microstates — from it flow free energy, entropy, pressure and every macroscopic thermodynamic quantity.

A Nobel-winning result that launched quantum theory, relating ejected electron energy to light frequency and work function — the basis of solar cells, photomultipliers and light sensors.

photon_energy.name: Photon Energy

Which equation gives the energy carried by a single particle of light from its frequency? 💡

A fundamental quantum relation linking a light quantum's energy to its frequency via a universal constant — underpinning spectroscopy, solar cells, lasers and the whole electromagnetic spectrum.

poisson_equation_v1.name: Poisson's Equation (Version 1)
poisson_equation_v1.caption:

A fundamental partial differential equation generalising the source-free case, connecting a potential to its charge or mass density — central to electrostatics, gravitation and steady-state physics.

poisson_equation_v2.name: Poisson's Equation (Version 2)

Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A workhorse partial differential equation relating field curvature to source distribution — appearing in gravitational potentials, electrostatic fields and semiconductor device modelling.

An idealisation in fluid dynamics describing irrotational, incompressible motion via a scalar velocity field — a powerful approximation for aerodynamics, water waves and streaming past bodies.

A key quantity in electromagnetism, giving the direction and rate of electromagnetic energy transport — describing how light, radio waves and power radiate and flow through space.

A practical concept in hydraulics, expressing pressure as the height of a fluid column via density and gravity — central to pump design, water supply systems and measuring pressure.

prony_equation.name: Prony Equation

Which ancient equation relates the three sides of a right triangle — geometry's most famous result? 📐

A foundational result in geometry, connecting the squares of a right triangle's sides — the basis of distance calculations, trigonometry, navigation and coordinate geometry across all of mathematics.

A cornerstone of algebra, giving the roots of any second-degree polynomial from its coefficients — indispensable in physics, engineering, economics and anywhere parabolas and optimisation appear.

A fundamental construct in quantum mechanics acting as a derivative on the wavefunction — the basis of the Hamiltonian, commutation relations and the mathematical formalism of the quantum world.

A fundamental limit in optics, linking angular resolution to wavelength and aperture — determining the sharpness of telescopes, microscopes and cameras through diffraction.

rayleigh_jeans_law.name: Rayleigh-Jeans Law
rayleigh_jeans_law.caption:

A classical result for blackbody radiation that fails at high frequencies — its 'ultraviolet catastrophe' exposed the limits of classical physics and helped trigger the quantum revolution.

A compact complex-field formulation of electromagnetism combining electric and magnetic fields — useful in the photon wavefunction, optics and elegant reformulations of the field equations.

Which function encodes the primes and sits at the heart of the greatest unsolved problem in mathematics? ➗

riemann_zeta_function_v2.caption:

A remarkable object in number theory linking nested fractions, integer partitions and modular forms — celebrated identities that continue to influence combinatorics and mathematical physics.

rotatum.caption:
Which quantity is the rate of change of torque — the rotational cousin of the rate of change of force? 🌀

rydberg_formula.name: Rydberg Formula
rydberg_formula.caption:
Which equation predicts the exact wavelengths of light emitted by hydrogen's glowing spectral lines? 🌈

A landmark of atomic spectroscopy, predicting the wavelengths of spectral lines from transitions between energy levels — a key clue that led to quantum models of the atom.

Which quantity sets the altitude over which an atmosphere's pressure or density drops by a factor of e? ⛰️

schrodinger_equation.name: Schrödinger Equation
schrodinger_equation.caption:
Which equation governs how a quantum wavefunction evolves in time — the F=ma of the quantum world? ⚛️

The foundational equation of quantum mechanics, describing how the wavefunction of a system evolves — the basis of atomic structure, chemistry, semiconductors and quantum technology.

Which equation gives the radius at which an object becomes a black hole, its escape speed reaching light? 🕳️

shannon_entropy.name: Shannon Entropy

Which equation measures information itself — how much surprise or uncertainty a message carries? 📡

The founding equation of information theory, quantifying uncertainty and information content — the basis of data compression, cryptography, communication channels and modern computing.

simple_harmonic_motion.name: Simple Harmonic Motion
simple_harmonic_motion.caption:
Which equation describes anything oscillating with a restoring force proportional to its displacement? 🔄

A foundational model of oscillations, giving sinusoidal motion for pendulums, springs and vibrating systems — the building block for waves, resonance and normal modes across physics.

snap_fourth_derivative.name: Snap (Fourth Derivative)
snap_fourth_derivative.caption:
Which quantity is the fourth time-derivative of position — the one right after jerk? 🎢

A higher-order kinematic quantity, the rate of change of jerk — important in motion planning, robotics and designing smooth acceleration profiles for machinery and vehicles.

snells_law.name: Snell's Law
snells_law.caption:
Which equation explains how light bends when it passes from air into water or glass? 🔦

A cornerstone of optics, relating the angles and refractive indices as light crosses between media — behind lenses, prisms, fibre optics and the shimmering of mirages.

standard_deviation.name: Standard Deviation
standard_deviation.caption:
Which statistic measures how spread out a set of data is around its average? 📊

A fundamental measure of dispersion in statistics, quantifying variability around the mean — essential to data analysis, quality control, finance and interpreting experimental uncertainty.

stefan_boltzmann_law.name: Stefan-Boltzmann Law
stefan_boltzmann_law.caption:
Which equation says a hot object's radiated power soars with the fourth power of its temperature? 🔥
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A key result in thermal radiation, giving the power radiated by a blackbody as temperature to the fourth power — central to stellar luminosity, climate balance and thermal engineering.

stokes_einstein_equation.name: Stokes-Einstein Equation
stokes_einstein_equation.caption:
Which equation predicts how fast a tiny particle diffuses through a fluid from its size and viscosity? 🔬

A bridge between statistical physics and fluid mechanics, relating a particle's diffusion coefficient to temperature, viscosity and radius — key to colloids, nanoparticles and molecular sizing.

stokes_law_v1.name: Stokes' Law (Version 1)
stokes_law_v1.caption:
Which equation gives the viscous drag on a tiny sphere creeping slowly through a fluid? 🔬

A classic result in low-Reynolds-number fluid dynamics, giving the drag on a small sphere from radius, viscosity and speed — behind sedimentation, aerosols and measuring fluid viscosity.

stokes_law_v2.name: Stokes' Law (Version 2)
stokes_law_v2.caption:
Which equation predicts the terminal speed of a small sphere settling under gravity in a viscous fluid? 🔬

thermodynamic_entropy.caption:
Which equation defines the change in a system's disorder from heat exchanged at a given temperature? 🌡️
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

A foundational definition in the physics of heat, relating this quantity’s change to reversible heat flow over temperature — the property that only ever increases and sets the arrow of time.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

toomre_stability_criterion.name: Toomre Stability Criterion
toomre_stability_criterion.caption:
Which equation decides whether a rotating disc of gas or stars will fragment and collapse under gravity? 🌌
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A key result in astrophysics, balancing self-gravity against pressure and rotation to predict disc instability — central to star formation, galactic spiral structure and protoplanetary discs.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

torque.name: Torque
torque.caption:
Which quantity measures the twisting effect of a force — why a longer wrench loosens a bolt more easily? 🔧
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A fundamental quantity in rotational mechanics, the rotational analogue of force, depending on force and lever arm — behind engines, gears, levers and everything that rotates.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

tsiolkovsky_rocket_equation.name: Tsiolkovsky Rocket Equation
tsiolkovsky_rocket_equation.caption:
Which equation reveals how a spacecraft's final speed depends on exhaust velocity and how much propellant it burns? 🚀
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

The founding equation of astronautics, relating change in velocity to exhaust speed and the ratio of initial to final mass — governing spacecraft propulsion, staging and the tyranny of the fuel budget.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

van_t_hoff_equation.name: van 't Hoff Equation
van_t_hoff_equation.caption:
Which equation predicts how a chemical equilibrium shifts as you change the temperature? ⚗️
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A key relation in physical chemistry, linking the temperature dependence of an equilibrium constant to reaction enthalpy — essential to reaction engineering, solubility and thermochemistry.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

vis_viva_equation.name: Vis Viva Equation
vis_viva_equation.caption:
Which equation gives an orbiting body's speed at any point along its elliptical path? 🛰️
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A fundamental result in orbital mechanics, relating orbital speed to distance and orbit size via energy conservation — indispensable for spacecraft trajectories, transfers and mission planning.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

vorticity_equation.name: Vorticity Equation
vorticity_equation.caption:
Which equation governs how the local spinning of a fluid — its swirl — is created and transported? 🌪️
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

A central equation in fluid dynamics describing how the local spinning of a flow is generated and transported — key to turbulence, tornadoes, aerodynamics and ocean and atmospheric circulation.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

wave_equation_v1.name: Wave Equation (Version 1)
wave_equation_v1.caption:
Which equation describes how disturbances propagate through space and time — sound, light and vibrating strings? 🌊
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A fundamental partial differential equation of physics, governing how disturbances travel at a fixed speed — describing sound, light, seismic motion, water ripples and vibrating strings.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

wave_equation_v2.name: Wave Equation (Version 2)
wave_equation_v2.caption:
Which equation, using the spacetime d'Alembertian, captures propagating disturbances in a relativistic form? 🌊
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A compact statement of how disturbances propagate, central to electromagnetism, acoustics and field theory — the mathematical backbone of nearly every propagating disturbance in physics.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

wien_displacement_law.name: Wien Displacement Law
wien_displacement_law.caption:
Which equation says hotter objects glow at shorter wavelengths — why stars shift from red to blue? 🌈
Name it in the comments, then swipe to reveal 👉

A key result in thermal radiation, relating the peak wavelength of a blackbody's glow to its temperature — behind stellar colours, infrared thermometers and thermal imaging.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

work_energy_theorem.name: Work-Energy Theorem
work_energy_theorem.caption:
Which equation says the net work done on an object equals its change in energy of motion? 🏋️
Drop your guess in the comments before you swipe 👉

A cornerstone of mechanics, connecting the work done by forces to the change in kinetic energy — a powerful shortcut for problems involving speed, forces and distance.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌

young_laplace_equation.name: Young-Laplace Equation
young_laplace_equation.caption:
Which equation explains the pressure jump across a curved liquid surface — why bubbles and droplets form? 🫧
Comment your answer before you swipe to check 👉

A foundational result in surface science, relating the pressure difference across an interface to surface tension and curvature — governing bubbles, droplets, capillarity and emulsions.

Save it to quiz a friend later 📌
